title: Determine the exact value of R(B13, B15)
lean_gate_reason: The verified claim is already theorem-shaped, reproducible, and close to a paper packet. Formalizing the counting lemmas and the strongly-regular spectral contradiction looks like the shortest remaining path to a sealed exact packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Verified candidate exact solve. The theorem R(B13, B15) = 57 is still paper-shaped rather than merely instance-shaped: the proof is short, reproducible, family-anchored, and would already supply most of a one-theorem note once Lean-sealed.
family_affinity: Strong. This is an almost-diagonal B_{n-2} versus B_n book Ramsey case, and the proof mechanism is family-facing rather than instance-only.
generalization_signal: Moderate-positive. Goodman plus integrality may transfer to nearby one-gap almost-diagonal book cases when the extremal graph is forced into a unique near-regular shape.
strongest_honest_claim: Assuming the literature lower bound 57 <= R(B13, B15), the local argument verifies that no 57-vertex graph can avoid B13 while its complement avoids B15; equivalently the candidate exact value is R(B13, B15) = 57.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any 57-vertex graph avoiding B13 and co-avoiding B15 would have to be strongly regular with parameters (57,28,12,15), which is spectrally impossible.
next_action: Advance to publication_audit with the verified candidate theorem packet, then formalize in Lean unless publication audit uncovers bounded-scope rediscovery.
